steps.title: Recall the standard form of a circle
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ satisfies:
$$(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$$
Note the radius is squared on the right side.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$(x-16)^2 + (y-17)^2 = 49k$': Uses $49k$ instead of $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$ — forgets to square the entire radius expression. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(x-16)^2 + (y-17)^2 = 49k^2$': Correctly squares the radius: $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$(x-16)^2 + (y-17)^2 = 7k$': Places the unsquared radius $7k$ on the right side. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(x-16)^2 + (y-17)^2 = 7x^2$': Introduces $x$ into the right side, which is not part of the radius. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
